D1 “Lovelace’s Loop” — plain Claude vs. our system

The same assignment — a car cresting a circular hill (Newton’s second law for circular motion) — produced two ways. Drag each divider to wipe between what a plain assistant generates and what the curriculum engine produces. Both sides are the real Microsoft Word rendering of each worksheet.

Before — Plain Claude, isolated: no repo, no library, and explicitly asked (as our engine is) for a native editable Word diagram. It built one. Its math is plain text. After — Our system: a calibrated editable DrawingML scene, typeset OMML math, and the LOL energy chart the course teaches.

The Problem

Page one of each worksheet — problem statement, scene diagram, and the questions. Both diagrams are real, editable Word shapes: the plain agent, asked for one, delivered.

Before · Plain Claude After · Our System

← drag to compare →

The Solution

The worked solution — where the two actually part ways. Plain Claude writes its math as plain text (v² / R) and labels the normal force N, so its own answer reads “N = 7,920 N.” The engine typesets real equation objects and carries the course’s notation.

Before · Plain Claude After · Our System

← drag to compare →

Rendered through Microsoft Word. Before: a plain isolated Claude agent (terse ask, no repo access, native-editable-diagram requirement stated). After: our engine (circular-D001), solution fit to one page.
